title: CubeSat Electrical Power System Adaptive MPPT Controller
description: A 1U CubeSat on-board EPS controller that implements adaptive Perturb-and-Observe MPPT on body-mounted solar panels, monitors battery state-of-charge via dual INA219 shunt sensors, performs sun-angle optimisation using the ICM-42688-P IMU feeding a cascaded PID attitude nudge signal, executes autonomous fault detection (over-voltage, over-current, low-battery) with load shedding, and logs all telemetry over USB-serial at 1 Hz. Differentiated from the existing CubeSat cold-gas RCS attitude-hold and propulsion adaptive-controller projects by focusing entirely on the electrical power subsystem and photovoltaic energy harvest rather than propulsion or attitude actuation.
objective: Maximise solar energy harvest on a 1U CubeSat via closed-loop MPPT; maintain battery voltage within 6.5–8.4 V safe operating band; detect and respond to EPS faults autonomously with load shedding; log continuous telemetry (V, I, power, duty, fault flag) at 1 Hz over serial.
constraints: Teensy 4.0 / STM32 Nucleo (ARM Cortex-M); two INA219 monitors; ICM-42688-P IMU; external synchronous buck converter; 2S Li-ion 2200 mAh; body-mounted 2 W solar panel array; no moving parts; factor of safety 1.5 on discharge current rating; I2C + SPI buses; USB-serial telemetry; total BOM < $35.
supporting_fact_titles: Maximum Power Point Tracking (MPPT) for Photovoltaic Sources
design_element_titles: Cascaded PID Rate and Attitude Controller

element_usage_notes.INA219 Bidirectional Current/Power Monit: Two INA219s sample solar panel and battery rails at 20 Hz; readings feed MPPT P&O logic, battery SoC estimation, and all three fault threshold checks.
element_usage_notes.Perturb-and-Observe MPPT Duty-Cycle Cont: Core energy-harvest loop; adjusts buck converter duty each 50 ms cycle based on dP/dD sign; overridden to reduced safe-mode duty on any fault condition.
element_usage_notes.ICM-42688-P High-Rate IMU via SPI: Reads ACCEL_Z at 20 Hz to estimate satellite solar-face orientation; az = 0.5 g target corresponds to optimal ~45° panel-to-sun incidence angle for body-mounted panels.
element_usage_notes.Cascaded PID Rate and Attitude Controlle: Outer PID on az error produces an attitude nudge PWM command routed to the spacecraft attitude system, orienting the solar face toward the sun to increase panel irradiance.

element_interactions.to: Cascaded PID Rate and Attitude Controller
element_interactions.description: IMU supplies Z-axis acceleration as a sun-angle proxy; PID drives error toward 0.5 g target representing optimal panel orientation.

element_interactions.from: Cascaded PID Rate and Attitude Controller
element_interactions.description: PID outputs attitude-correction signal; MPPT operates in full P&O mode when az is near target; holds last duty during large slew manoeuvres.
Real-time V/I measurements are the primary inputs to the MPPT power calculation and all fault threshold checks.
MPPT adjusts buck converter duty, changing the solar panel operating point that the INA219 reads back on the next measurement cycle.
